reference: Hello. A burning sensation in the urinary tract or urethra can most commonly be due to either an infection of the bladder or urethra, or any stone problem, which may have passed in the bladder-kidney system causing infection and thus burning. It can also be due to unprotected sexual intercourse, which leads to an infection of the urethra. The diagnosis of this condition is important before treatment is initiated. Urine microscopy and sonography of the abdomen and pelvis are initial urine and scanning modalities that are required for diagnosis. A physical examination by a urologist is also of paramount importance as it is often the best modality to establish the diagnosis. Therefore, I would advise you to take plenty of fluids for now, as repeated urination can help wash out the infection. However, the aforementioned tests and physical examination are mandatory in your case. Since you also have pain when passing semen, it could be a prostate infection, which again requires a per rectal examination by a urologist to assess the status of the prostate.
